steps.title: Find the zeros
steps.content: The question asks for a form that displays the zeros. In Desmos, you can enter values from $f(x)=x^2-42x+80$ and regress them into factored form $(x-r)(x-s)$. The fitted values reveal the zeros $2$ and $40$.

steps.title: Scan the choices for 2 and 40 as visible literals
steps.content:
Factoring by inspection gives the same result: two numbers that multiply to $80$ and add to $-42$ are $-2$ and $-40$, so
$$f(x)=(x-2)(x-40)$$
That form displays the zeros $x=2$ and $x=40$ as constants, which matches choice B.
